channel.title: Karam's Blog
channel.description: Thoughts on development, design, and technology

item.title: Book Summary: Growing Object-Oriented Software, Guided by Tests
item.description: I have finally gotten round to reading the famous GOOS book that has been around since 2009. Here is my summary.

item.title: Five Minute Design Patterns #3: The Prototype Pattern
item.description: Another post in my blog post series where I try to cover all of the Gang of Four Design Patterns in five minutes each, once again we have one of the Creational Patterns... the Prototype Pattern!

item.title: Passing your PSM1 & becoming a certified Scrum Master.
item.description: I give you my top tips on how you can pass your Scrum.org PSM1 exam to become a fully certified Scrum Master.

item.title: Five Minute Design Patterns #2: The Factory Pattern
item.description: The second post in my blog post series where I try to cover all of the Gang of Four Design Patterns in five minutes each, this time it's one of the Creational Patterns... the Factory Pattern!

item.title: Five Minute Design Patterns #1: The Builder Pattern
item.description: The first of posts in my blog post series where I try to cover all of the Gang of Four Design Patterns in five minutes each, this time it's one of the Creational Patterns... the Builder Pattern!

item.title: Easily test your Vuex store using Vue Test Utils.
item.description: Tired of brittle tests by testing your Vuex actions, mutations and getters all separately? Vue Test Utils has the solution!

item.title: A common enemy to software developers.
item.description: Fear of missing out & imposter syndrome are two serious problems that many software developers have to deal with. Trust me, you're not alone... & trust me, you're also fantastic at what you do, regardless what you might think.

item.title: Tips for learning a new code base.
item.description: Need to quickly learn a new code base or upskill in some technologies? Here are my tips.

item.title: Test-driven Development: A step by step example of the Roman Numerals Kata
item.description: Understanding TDD is one thing, but putting it into practice is another!

item.title: Creating & deploying a .NET Core Web API to AWS with the Serverless Framework.
item.description: Lambda functions are nice... but sometimes you just want a whole API!

item.title: Running Entity Framework Core migrations from a separate solution that doesn't contain a Startup.cs file.
item.description: After an hour of tearing my hair out...

item.title: Updating your .gitignore
item.description: When you forget to add a .gitignore to your project...

item.title: Microservices 101 ft. DDD: The good, the bad & the ugly.
item.description: I attended the latest Designing Microservices workshop at NDC London 2019...

item.title: Data Visualisation, The Dark Web and Lightning Talks... Interested?
item.description: Does this title incite you? Well, I have the solution on Tuesday March 19th!

item.title: Deploying an AdonisJS application to the cloud using AWS Elastic Beanstalk.
item.description: From your hard drive to the AWS cloud in seconds!

item.title: My Raspberry Pi Zero & the USB Audio Confusion
item.description: So... I won a free Raspberry Pi Zero from Google! Then I bought some USB speakers for it and chaos ensued!

item.title: Associating templating file formats with html in Visual Studio Code
item.description: A simple solution, to an annoying problem.

item.title: AdonisJS, a match made in heaven?
item.description: Discussing our challenge for December 2018...

item.title: The best thing since []bread?
item.description: Our monthly challenge for November 2018 is complete...

item.title: Deploying a .NET Core Application to a $5 DigitalOcean droplet using Dokku
item.description: Oceans, docks, whales & five dollars.

item.title: My ReSharper-esque Visual Studio Code bindings
item.description: Used to ReSharper bindings like me? Get 'em in VS Code!.

item.title: Turning iTerm in to a Quake style terminal on Mac OS
item.description: Like ConEmu on Windows? Get the same on Mac OS!

item.title: Connection Strings in .NET Core
item.description: When you waste an entire hour trying to define a connection string...

item.title: Dependency Injection in .NET Core
item.description: I think it's safe to say that there's a lot of hype around .NET Core nowadays because of it being cross-platform, open source, etc, which isn't really something we've been used to from Microsoft technologies over the years. So as someone who works in a predominantly .NET dominated environment, I wanted to dedicate some time playing around with it, building a simple API & just investigating how different it is to the regular .NET Framework.

item.title: Removing conflicted copies on Dropbox
item.description: As someone who uses Dropbox on pretty much all the different gazillion devices I own, I came across a rather frustrating issue. For some reason, almost every time my Macbook Pro would try to synchronise files modified on one of my Windows devices with Dropbox, meaning the older versions still on my Macbook would need updating, I'd end up with a 'conflicted copy' version of that file being generated by Dropbox.
